and professional staff that are not administrators.

[05:30–06:00]
Administrators. This allotment is about $1.2 million. There's a third allotment of money in House

[06:30–07:00]
that one of the main reasons educators are leaving the profession is due to is the behavior of some

highlight a few things. It allows teachers to remove students from the classroom who repeatedly, and
there will have to be rules around what repeatedly means. We're going to have to wait for guidance
on that, but who repeatedly interfere with the teachers.

[07:00–07:30]
Interfere with the teacher's ability to communicate effectively with the students in the class or
the students' ability to learn. It removes the limit on in school suspension days from a maximum of
three, which is what we've been under previously, to there's no maximum. Again, I'm sure there will
be rules around that, how that has to happen, but that's just the verbiage of the of the bill. It
adds exceptions to the out-of-school suspension ban for students in grades three or lower. So
previously we've we've had restrictions on what we can do with kids that are in grades three or
lower. Now the bill.

[07:30–08:00]
states that there are some exceptions to students who make threats to the immediate health and
safety of other students in the classroom. That's a direct quote. And who conduct that significant
con whose conduct significantly disrupts the classroom. So again, all of that will have to be

of-school suspension that it one that their child might receive be an in-school suspension if they

behind
